---
title: "BROWT"
subtitle: "Learning to play Brawl Stars from pixels alone"
---

## Abstract

This report documents the design and evaluation of an agent that plays Brawl
Stars **using only what is on the screen** — no access to the game's internal
state. A frame is captured, objects are detected, the detections are turned into
a state, and a reinforcement-learning policy chooses an action that is sent back
to the game. The change in the resulting frames is the reward.

The aim of these pages is **scientific validation**: to expose the results
obtained at each stage and the method used to obtain them, rather than to teach
how to operate the tool.

- **Task studied:** Solo Showdown (MVP scope).
- **Approach:** perception (YOLOv11) → state → reinforcement learning, closed in
  a perception-action loop.
- **Status:** the perception stage is under evaluation; later stages are work in
  progress and will be reported as results arrive.

## Hypothesis

A purely vision-based agent, with no privileged access to game internals, can
learn a useful Solo Showdown policy if the perception stage produces a state
that is accurate and stable enough for a reinforcement-learning policy to act
on.

## Method in one diagram

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    F[Frame] -->|detection -> state| G[RL policy]
    G -->|action| H[Game]
    H --> F
    H -.reward.-> G
```

Each stage is studied on its own tab: how it was built (method) and what it
achieves (results). The [Architecture](architecture.qmd) tab gives the full
picture; the per-stage tabs report the evidence.

## How to read this report

The tabs follow the perception-to-action pipeline. Stages still being evaluated
are marked **work in progress** and will be filled in as their results become
available.
